creator: freebsd@nagilum.org
text:
nagilum@cakebox ~/Projects/C/src/a16 > clang -O2 -o a16.clang-O2 a16.c
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: /usr/bin/clang -cc1 -triple i386-unknown-freebsd9.0 -emit-obj -disable-free -main-file-name a16.c -mrelocation-model static -mdisable-fp-elim -masm-verbose -mconstructor-aliases -target-cpu i486 -momit-leaf-frame-pointer -resource-dir /usr/bin/../lib/clang/3.0 -fmodule-cache-path /var/tmp/clang-module-cache -O2 -ferror-limit 19 -fmessage-length 80 -fgnu-runtime -fobjc-runtime-has-arc -fobjc-runtime-has-weak -fobjc-fragile-abi -fdiagnostics-show-option -fcolor-diagnostics -o /tmp/a16-nmIQSw.o -x c a16.c
1.      <eof> parser at end of file
2.      Code generation
3.      Running pass 'Function Pass Manager' on module 'a16.c'.
4.      Running pass 'X86 DAG->DAG Instruction Selection' on function '@main'
clang: error: unable to execute command: Illegaler Speicherzugriff: 11 (core dumped)
clang: error: clang frontend command failed due to signal 2 (use -v to see invocation)
clang: note: diagnostic msg: Please submit a bug report to http://llvm.org/bugs/ and include command line arguments and all diagnostic information.
clang: note: diagnostic msg: Preprocessed source(s) are located at:
clang: note: diagnostic msg: /tmp/a16-6WzhFB.i
nagilum@cakebox ~/Projects/C/src/a16 > clang --version
FreeBSD clang version 3.0 (tags/RELEASE_30/final 145349) 20111210
Target: i386-unknown-freebsd9.0
Thread model: posix

pid 89559 (clang), uid 1000: exited on signal 11 (core dumped)

Fix: uhm..for me it's stick with gcc ;)
How-To-Repeat: 	It doesn't really seem to matter what program I try to compile,
	the system clang just coredumps even without any -O option.
	The ports version(also 3.0) works though.
